old-southern-chocolate-pecan-sheet-cake.name: Old Southern Chocolate Pecan Sheet Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease bottom and sides of a 9x13-inch pan.
Mix 2 cups white sugar, flour, and salt in a mixing bowl.

Combine water, vegetable shortening, margarine, and 3 tablespoons cocoa powder in a saucepan. Bring mixture to a boil over medium heat, stirring frequently. Pour liquid into flour mixture and stir well. Return saucepan to stove and set it aside.

Dissolve baking soda in buttermilk in a large liquid measuring cup and stir in eggs and vanilla extract. Pour egg mixture into cake batter and mix until fully combined. Transfer batter into the prepared pan.
Bake the cake in preheated oven until cake springs back when touched lightly with a finger or a tester comes out clean, 25 to 30 minutes.

While cake is baking, prepare icing using reserved saucepan. Combine confectioners' sugar, 3 tablespoons cocoa powder, butter, milk, vanilla, and pecans in saucepan. Cook and stir over medium heat until hot but not boiling. Pour icing over hot cake immediately after it comes out of the oven. Allow cake to cool completely on a wire rack.

olive-pie-for-fasting-argiro.name: Olive Pie For Fasting
instructions: In a bowl, add the olive oil, 2 cup orange juice, fresh and dried mint.
Dissolve the soda in the remaining (1 cup) orange juice and add to the other ingredients.
Add the fresh and dry onions and mix.
Add the baking powder to the flour and mix the dough with wire.
Add the olives and smooth tomatoes. In a spoon, mix to dress with flour.
Add the solid materials to the mixture with the liquid materials and mix gently with a spoon.
Add the nuts and mix gently.
We oil very well a shallow 32 cm baking pan or fireproof.
Spread the mixture in layer. We're straightening the surface.
Sprinkle with sesame and pumpkin seed.
Bake in a well preheated oven at 170 degrees C for about 40 minutes.
After baking, let it stand 10 minutes on the baking pan and cut into pieces.

one-pot-chicken-alfredo-soup.name: One-pot Chicken Alfredo Soup

ingredients.prep: skinless chicken breasts, cut into  0.5-inch cubes
plus 2 tablespoons, plus more for garnish

instructions: In a medium bowl, season the chicken with the salt and pepper and toss to coat.

In a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat until shimmering. Working in batches, cook the chicken, turning the pieces halfway through, until lightly browned on all sides, 5–6 minutes per batch. If the pot seems dry, add more olive oil as needed between batches. Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.

Reduce the heat to medium and add the butter. Once melted, add the shallots. Cook for 2–3 minutes, until translucent. Add the garlic and cook for 1–2 minutes, until fragrant. Add the flour, stirring to coat the aromatics. Cook for 1–2 minutes, until the flour is just cooked but has not yet started to brown.

Pour the white wine into the pot to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Add the chicken stock and season with salt. Increase the heat to medium-high and bring to a boil. Once boiling, stir in the fettuccine and cook for 7 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent the pasta from sticking to the bottom of the pot or clumping together.

Add the chicken and heavy cream, stirring to combine. Add the Parmesan cheese, a couple tablespoons at a time, stirring well between each addition to ensure the cheese melts smoothly. Stir in the spinach and cook for 1–2 minutes, until wilted. Season with salt and pepper to taste. The soup will thicken slightly as it cools.
Ladle into bowls and serve warm, garnished with more pepper and Parmesan cheese.
Enjoy!

orange-and-chocolate-chip-muffins-argiro.name: Orange And Chocolate Chip Muffins

Bake the orange muffins in a well preheated oven at 170oC in the air on the medium grill for about 25 minutes.
We check if they were baked in the center by sinking a toothpick before removing them from the oven.
Allow them to stand for 10 minutes in the form and then dissipate.

orange-cream-milk-punch.name: Orange Cream Milk Punch

Pour in the milk and lemon-lime soda.
Stir gently and serve immediately.

orange-duck-traditional-french-recipe-pe.name: Orange Duck, Traditional French Recipe
orange-duck-traditional-french-recipe-pe.original_name: Pato a la Naranja, receta francesa tradicional

ingredients.item: 1/2 taza de grand marnier o cointreau
Sal y pimienta negra al gusto
1/2 taza de vinagre de vino blanco
2 cucharadas de grand marnier o cointreau (opcional)

instructions: Wash and dry the duck carefully. Be sure to remove any excess fat. Make small incisions to the skin of the duck without reaching the flesh. That will help the fat melt while cooking and the skin gets more crisp. Serve the duck with salt and pepper, both outside and inside.

In a big bowl, it combines the juice and fringes of 2 oranges, the Grand Marnier or Cointreau, the brown sugar, salt and pepper. Mix well to create a marinade. Put the duck in a fountain big enough to hold it and put the tipple onto it. Make sure that the duck's well covered by the marines. Cover with film paper and refrigerates for at least 4 hours or ideally throughout the night. Turn the duck occasionally to make sure it's tipsy and tipsy.

It purifies the oven at 180 and 3. Retire the pate from the tidal and scrape it, saving the tidal for the salsa. Put the duck into an oven plate and satir with salt and pepper. Bake the duck for about 2 hours, or until the skin is golden and crispy, and the juices come clear when you pinch them at the thigh's thigh. Turn the duck occasionally while cooking to make sure that it's cooked evenly.

While the duck's cooking, you can cook the orange sauce. In a saucepan, it combines the juice and fringes of oranges, sugar and vinegar. Take the mixture to boiling and then reduce low and cook with low heat until the salsa is reduced and thick about 15-20 minutes. Add chicken and liquor (if you wish) to salsa and cook for another 5 minutes. Adjust the date with salt and pepper as you prefer.
Once the duck is cooked, remove it from the oven and let it stand for about 10 minutes before cutting. Cut the duck into portions and serve with the orange sauce above.

orange-blossom-honey-madeleines.name: Orange-blossom-honey Madeleines
instructions: Sift flour, baking powder, and salt into a medium bowl. Using an electric mixer, beat eggs and sugar in a large bowl until pale yellow and thick, about 4 minutes. Beat in honey, vanilla, and orange zest. Gently fold in dry ingredients. Add 5 Tbsp. melted butter; gently fold into batter. Press plastic wrap directly onto surface of batter; chill for at least 3 hours. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

Position a rack in center of oven; preheat to 400°. Lightly brush madeleine molds with butter. Place pan on a baking sheet. Drop 1 scant Tbsp. batter into each mold. Bake until golden and a tester inserted into center comes out clean, about 10 minutes.
Remove pan from oven, invert, and quickly knock madeleines out of pan. Serve warm on their own or with Ginger-Yogurt Mousse with Pistachio Meringue.

orzo-with-sun-dried-tomatoes-and-kalamat.name: Orzo With Sun-dried Tomatoes And Kalamata Olives
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook orzo in the boiling water, stirring occasionally until cooked through but firm to the bite, about 11 minutes. Drain.

Mix orzo pasta, tomatoes, and olives together in a large bowl. Pour pesto over pasta mixture and stir to coat. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese over the pasta mixture; stir. Drizzle olive oil over the salad and stir to achieve your preferred moisture level.
